Yasuko Agawa was so sought after that she would sing at five different jazz clubs in a single night

A Mexican government agency paid suppliers in full upfront, then deliberately avoided requesting delivery of the goods

The oldest known sexually reproducing organism emerged during the Stenian period

Abdulhussain Abdulredha was nearly assassinated for his depiction of Saddam Hussein in Saif al-Arab

scored.topic: A Bill Gates funded mosquito factory in Medellín, Colombia, produces 40 million mosquitoes weekly for release via drones and bikes. These insects carry a natural bacterium that prevents them from transmitting viruses to humans. By mating with wild populations, they spread this trait.
This is how the ruins are displayed in Serbia

Horses are unguligrade animals, meaning they walk on the tips of their digits rather than the soles of their feet. What looks like a horse's leg below the knee is actually mostly one elongated middle finger ending in the hoof.

The 'Great Male Renunciation' was a period in fashion in the late 18th century where men in the West ceased wearing bright colors, jewelry, and varied garments in favor of utilitarian clothes and black suits.
Guests painting an entire mural
Foding Palace, Nanjing — one of the most insane Buddhist temples

Reliable sources are split as to whether the title of best-selling video game ever should belong to Tetris or Minecraft. Some consider it to be Tetris by combining the sales of all of its different versions (520M), while others consider it to be Minecraft (350M), rejecting the former's reasoning.
In Thai culture, feet are considered the dirtiest parts of the body and pointing at things or people with them is rude.
Blue whales have around 1,000 times more cells than humans do, yet they don't appear to suffer from higher cancer rates — a mystery that scientists call Peto's Paradox.
H. David Werder sat on a pole for 439 days, 11 hours, and 6 minutes in the 1980s to protest the price of gasoline.
The Netherlands sits below sea level and uses 3,500 pumping stations to keep the country dry.

Peter Benchley, the author of Jaws, wrote 3 versions of the script for Spielberg, all rejected or heavily edited. He got into an argument with Spielberg about the huge explosion at the end being unrealistic — and was thrown off the set.

The NBA shot clock was introduced in response to extremely low-scoring games, including the infamous November 22, 1950 matchup when the Fort Wayne Pistons defeated the Minneapolis Lakers 19–18; the fourth quarter score was only 3–1.
A mama bear scaring away a tiger with a cub behind her in Tadoba, India

Margarine was colored yellow to improve sales in the 1880s. Originally white and resembling lard, the dairy industry lobbied to ban yellow coloring. The W.E. Dennison Company sold margarine with a capsule of yellow dye for consumers to mix themselves in response to the bans.
Aerial view of Pripyat, Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine

The 'Dark Phoenix Saga' was originally meant to end with Jean Grey being de-powered and returning to normal life. Marvel editor-in-chief Jim Shooter disagreed, believing her actions were too horrible to let her off the hook.
The ampersand '&' is actually a ligature of the Latin word 'et,' meaning 'and.' Therefore, 'etc.' can also be written as '&c.'
Wisteria Wall in Ashikaga Flower Park, Japan
Siberian Whiskered Bats can live to be more than 40 years old, making them the longest-living bat species in the world.
There are an estimated 3 million rats in New York City — nearly a third as many as the human population.

Once home to over 1,000 people, the Romanian village of Geamăna was flooded with toxic mining waste after 1982 to create a tailings pond for Cupru Min Abrud. Today, only the church tower remains visible above the polluted red lake.

One of the most extreme forms of parental care observed in nature is when the mother is consumed by her own offspring. Known as matriphagy, it has been documented in some species of insects, worms, arachnids, and amphibians.
Yo-yos are at least 2,400 years old, with ancient Greek pottery dated to 440 BCE showing a boy playing with one.
Nepalese Prime Minister Mathabarsingh was personally killed by his own nephew, Jung Bahadur, who himself became Prime Minister a year later after killing another prime minister.
The only non-American Martian rover, Zhurong, took a selfie of itself with its lander by dropping a separate detachable camera on the ground and then driving back to pose.
The idea that the Illuminati runs the world was accidentally invented in the 1960s.
A recent experience with ChatGPT 5.5 Pro
Bun's experimental Rust rewrite hits 99.8% test compatibility on Linux x64 glibc
'Humanol' was a real pharmaceutical product made from liquefied human fat.
